subject: DevOps
title: Signed Commits and Artifacts
description: A fast breakdown of Signed Commits and Artifacts for builders.

hook.text: Someone pushed code under a colleague's name. Git allows that. Without signing, you can't verify who.

buildup.text: Signing uses crypto keys to prove authorship. A signed commit guarantees it came from the key owner.

discovery.text: GPG and SSH signing work with Git. GitHub and GitLab show a Verified badge on signed commits.

twist.text: Artifact signing goes further. Sign Docker images and binaries so recipients can verify no tampering.

climax.text: Sigstore and Cosign make signing easy and keyless. Sign with your identity, verify via public logs.

punchline.text: Sign code and artifacts. Prove authenticity from commit to deploy.

quiz.question: Why should developers sign their Git commits?
options: To cryptographically prove who authored the code
